rounds.story: 🚶‍♂️ You're just 16 meters away — Covent Garden Piazza is a 5-minute stroll right in front of you. This square isn't just a pretty face; it's a living, breathing artwork with a centuries-old tale to tell. Originally laid out in 1631 by the father of English architecture, Inigo Jones, this was London's first public square — a bold experiment in urban living. The fruits of the Earl of Bedford's vision were so stunning that they drew visitors from across the country. Yet behind this orderly facade hides a ruder history: it's said that in its early years, the square was thick with bear baiting pits and brothels. 🎭 Now a foodie haven with over 100 eateries and cafes, it's easy to miss the whispers of its past — but keep your eyes peeled for the discreet memorials and artworks that hint at the piazza's layered story. 📍 Just 10 meters from here is **St Paul's Covent Garden**, the church that became the heart of British theatre, where actors once claimed it was haunted and would say their prayers before curtain up to ward off the spirits. [📖 Learn more about the Actors Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Paul%27s,_Covent_Garden) — their stained glass might have more drama than a West End play. 🗺️ From here, follow the cobblestones of King Street to the next chapter of your story.
